observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to:
1. Update and reconcile the Cashbook balances with the Ledger balances of the CIB accounts and promptly communicate to the Municipal Accountant any discrepancies identified for inclusion in the BRS or for proper adjustments in the accounting records; and
2. Cancel the stale checks and furnish the accountant with the duplicate copies of corresponding DVs and their supporting documents, for the preparation of the JEV following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of cancellation; and to call the attention or inform the payees of those canceled checks to surrender the stale checks to her office so she could issue new checks.
Municipal Accountant to regularly prepare and submit a three-way the BRS for all active bank accounts maintained by the Municipality to ensure the reconciliation of balances among the ledger, cashbook and bank records, and prevent the accumulation of variances; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly coordinate and reconcile their records related to the CIB accounts to ensure the accuracy of balances and support the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We reiterated the prior years’ recommendations that the Municipal Mayor:
Enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer to assist the Municipal Accountant in tracing the necessary documents and reconstructing the transaction history of the subject dormant receivables, in order to establish their validity and determine the appropriate accounting treatment;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize a provision for doubtful accounts for dormant receivables that have been determined to have no or low probability of collection, and ensure that adequate disclosures regarding such provision are included in the Notes to Financial Statements, in accordance with applicable accounting standards; and
Request authority to write-off of the identified dormant receivables in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if applicable, by directing the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the necessary supporting documents to COA to facilitate the request.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the SB to pass a resolution authorizing the transfer of properties to various recipients, and prepare Deeds of Donation for the actual transfer and acceptance by the latter, to substantiate the transfer of ownership of the said properties.

Thereafter, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the following journal entries to fairly present the PPE accounts:

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
Properly recognize the semi-expendable property and the corresponding expense accounts by using the list of new accounts as provided under Annex B of COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024;
Coordinate with the GSO-designate in tracing the items included in the Furniture and Fixtures account, amounting to ₱63,983.80, and accordingly, reclassify the items costing below ₱50,000.00 to semi-expendable expense, if any.
Prepare the following correcting entries:
Retrospectively apply the setting of new capitalization threshold of 50,000.00 by recording in the books the following adjusting journal entries:
General Fund Books
Special Education Fund Books

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GSO-designate and the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022, by ensuring the preparation and maintenance of the prescribed forms, registry, and reports to strengthen the controls and safeguards over semi-expendable properties.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO), consider the hiring of additional personnel to support the operations of the Accounting Office and GSO, subject to budget availability, to ensure full compliance with regulatory and audit requirements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Familiarize himself with the procedures for restating prior period FS and stay updated with the latest disclosure requirements and applicable accounting standards; and
Restate the CY 2023 FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to:
Ensure that the under-obligated amount of ₱9,895,355.24 is not carried over to the CY 2025 SCBAA under continuing appropriations, and revise the SCBAA accordingly to reflect the necessary adjustments;
Thereafter, fully obligate the total amount of all perfected contracts under the Actual Amounts column of the SCBAA to ensure the faithful representation of the Municipality’s financial position, in accordance with applicable accounting standards and budgetary principles, to enhance the accuracy of reported budget utilization and promote better transparency and accountability in financial reporting; and
Remain informed of current guidelines related to the preparation of the SCBAA to ensure accuracy and compliance with applicable financial reporting standards.

We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD focal person to:
Ensure that the PPAs to be implemented are in accordance with PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and other GAD-related provisions;
Facilitate the update and maintenance of the GAD Database using sex-disaggregated data from the Community-Based Monitoring System and other relevant sources, in compliance with Annex B and Section C.2 of the JMC to ensure that identified gender issues are evidence-based and reflective of actual conditions; and
Avoid including GAD-related sports PPAs that are not focused on the involvement of women, specifically elderly women, women with disabilities, and indigenous women.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
LSB to:
Provide copies of the approved annual SEF Budget to the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer, copy furnished to audit team, within seven (7) days from its approval as their basis for disbursement or certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes;
Submit quarterly and annual SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1, as prepared by the Municipal Accountant, to the DepEd CO, SB of Bagulin, La Union, and the Regional Offices of DBM and DILG not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter and not later than the 15th day of February of the following year. If not provided, request from the Office of the Municipal Accountant immediately; and
Ensure that the utilization report of the SEF Budget is posted in at least three (3) conspicuous public places for transparency and accountability in compliance with the FDP of the DILG.
Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer to religiously authorize, process, and report SEF transactions using the approved annual SEF Budget. Otherwise, hold transaction processing until such is provided by the LSB.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LSB ensure compliance of the SEF Budget with Item 5.2.2 of JC No. 1, s. 2017, specifically presenting the PAPs by allotment class and per school, using the prescribed SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1 (Annex A) in subsequent budget years.  Additionally, the use of lump-sum appropriations or generic appropriations should be strictly avoided to uphold the principles of transparency, equity, and responsiveness in the delivery of educational support services.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Municipal Budget Officer to actively assist the LSB in ensuring that SEF budgets incorporate the additional allowable expenses enumerated under Item 1.0 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2020, thereby promoting the holistic and inclusive utilization of the SEF for the benefit of all public school learners in the Municipality.

We recommended the Management, along with the accountable officers, to refund the amount of ₱2,005,000.00 due to the absence of legal basis and non-compliance with DBM Budget Circular No. 2024-4 and PSLMC Resolution No. 9, series of 2023.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Dutifully and promptly compute the shares of the ten barangays; and
Ensure the timely remittance of said shares within five (5) days after the end of each quarter, in strict compliance with Section 271(d) of the LGC, as amended.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, being the HoPE, to:
1. Require the BAC and BAC Secretariat to:
a. Use the updated Financial Bid Forms, Contract Agreement and the Omnibus Sworn Statement, ensuring that all mandatory provisions are included in accordance with the latest issuances of the GPPB, to avoid future disqualification of bidders; and
b. Regularly monitor the GPPB website for updates to remain informed of any revisions, issuances or guidelines that may affect procurement processes and documentation requirements.
2. Direct the Municipal Engineer to:
a. Prepare and submit all required Detailed Engineering Documents prior to the approval of the POW: and
b. Coordinate closely with the BAC to ensure that all the essential technical documents are complete, accurate and properly submitted, thereby ensuring that the project’s objectives, scope and expected outputs are clearly defined and effectively attained.

We reiterated our prior audit recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Engineer to strictly require the contractors to conduct materials and quality testing for all infrastructure projects to ensure that substandard materials are not used, and that all projects meet the quality standards prescribed by the DPWH;
Municipal Administrator to formulate and institutionalize internal policies prescribing specific sanctions or penalties for contractors who fail to comply with the required submission of materials and quality test results, ensuring accountability and enforcement of quality standards; and
Municipal Accountant to strictly require the submission of complete supporting documents, including materials and quality test results, as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001, prior to the processing and payment of any infrastructure project.

We reiterated our prior recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to ensure strict compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 regarding the installation of project signboards for all infrastructure projects. These signboards must accurately include all required project information and be prominently displayed in conspicuous locations within the municipal premises, and project sites, immediately upon award of the contract.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Administrator to formulate and implement an internal policy that clearly defines the sanctions or penalties to be imposed on contractors and personnel responsible for noncompliance with the requirement for the installation of project signboards to reinforce accountability and ensure adherence to COA Circular No. 2013-004.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
1. BAC to:
a. Strictly observe the submission of complete and accurate documentary requirements, including the APP and corresponding supplemental plans, certified and attached to the DVs; b. Ensure that bidding documents, particularly the BDS and Technical Specifications, are properly filled out and tailored for each PPAs; c. Indicate the bidding document fees in the ITB and require attachment of ORs as proof of payment; d. Adhere to the mandatory notification to all participating and losing bidders within the prescribed timeframe; e. Comply with the required timelines for the posting of the NOA and NTP on the PhilGEPS website; f. Ensure that pre-bid conferences are conducted for all procurements with an ABC of ₱1,000,000.00 and above, and that the corresponding minutes are promptly recorded, maintained, and made available to stakeholders in compliance with Section 22 of the IRR; g. Strictly enforce the requirement that PSs are posted prior to contract signing; h. Require the inclusion of germination testing or germination rate in the technical specifications for the procurement of seeds; and i. Ensure that a DOLE-approved CSHP is submitted prior to the commencement of any construction activity, in compliance with DO 198, s. 2018, and Section 37.2.3 of the 2016 revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
2. Municipal Accountant to:
a. Release the retention money only upon submission of the acceptable substitutes; and b. Strictly enforce the checklist of documentary requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001, ensuring that no payments are processed without complete and proper documentation.
3. Municipal Engineer to:
a. Strictly adhere to the provisions of DPWH DO No. 197 in computing the ABC and secure proper approvals from the MPDC and the Municipal Mayor; and b. Enforce all SCC provisions, especially those related to As-Built Plans, and ensure compliance with relevant COA and DPWH issuances before signing the final accomplishments.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Evaluate personnel workloads to handle the above-mentioned additional responsibilities.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive directs the officers and employees concerned to facilitate the submission of receipts and disbursement vouchers (SEF and Trust Fund) together with its supporting documents to avoid suspension and disallowance in audit.  Further, it is recommended that the accountable personnel ensure compliance to COA Circular No. 2009-006 and PD 1445 to enable the timely review and evaluation of the municipality’s financial transactions as to propriety, completeness and accuracy.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents as shown in the attached Annex A.  Thereafter, the ICO-Municipal Accountant being the one certifying the completeness of documents in the disbursement vouchers, to see to it that all claims are duly supported before effecting payment.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the ICO-Municipal Accountant to submit immediately the lacking supporting documents as shown above to avoid suspension and/or disallowances in audit.

We recommended Management to:
Require the ICO-Municipal Accountant to submit the required documentary requirements as provided by COA Circular No. 2012-001 and to ensure that the succeeding transactions were properly documented before effecting payments; and
Record the financial assistance or aid as Due from LGUs and Due from NGAs and then closed to the Subsidy to LGUs and NGAs accounts when fund utilization report and other pertinent documents were submitted to ensure that the particular assistance was used for its intended purpose.

Further, the ICO-Municipal Accountant strictly comply with the corresponding procedures prescribed in Section 22, Volume I of the MNGAS and COA Circular No. 2015-009 on the proper accounting treatment of discounts in the collection of taxes for fair presentation thereof in the financial statements.

Adhere to the principles of Imprest System in maintaining the Petty Cash Fund set forth under Section 48, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; and
Refrain from using the Petty Cash Fund for the payment of regular expenditures like the Financial Assistance, internet expenses, office supplies, traveling and gasoline expenses which could be paid out of other regular disbursement process.

We recommend further that for fair presentation of receivables, Management assess the actual status of the dormant receivables; confirm with existing debtors and request for write-off in accordance with Commission on Audit Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if applicable.
Management is also enjoined to adhere with the general and specific guidelines cited in COA Circular No. 2016-005 in order to minimize the incidence of receivables falling past due.

Strictly adhere to Section 55 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs) Volume II and steps “i to k” of the Check Disbursement Process on Section 44 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184, or the Act providing for the Modernization, Standardization and Regulation of Procurement Activities of the Government and for Other Purposes be strictly adhered to by the Municipality to avoid administrative and criminal sanctions.

We reiterated our recommendation to require contractors to post the required warranty security in any acceptable form for completed projects in accordance with Section 62 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 and to establish a system to maintain records on specific warranty periods and security for each procurement of civil works.  We have further recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Inspectorate Team to conduct inspection of completed projects after the one (1) year defects liability period and issue the corresponding Certificate of Final Acceptance to the contractors if during the inspection, no defect/deficiency was detected.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that in order to validate the existence,
reliability and accuracy as well as custody and status of agency assets as well as to
establish proper valuation, sufficient control and accountability for each property,
Management evaluate the workloads of personnel and ensure that:
 The members of the Inventory Committee conduct annual physical inventory and
thereafter prepare and submit the RPCPPE for adjustment in the agency
books/records, if necessary; or otherwise undertake a reconstitution of the
committee;
 Personnel assigned at the Treasury Office maintain Property Cards and keep
inventory records updated starting with the recent acquisitions and disposals; and
 The Accounting personnel maintain the PPE Ledger cards and regularly
reconcile the same with the inventory records maintained by the Treasury Office.

We recommended that in order to enforce collection, Management send demand
letters to the last known addresses of the borrowers and thereafter exhaust all
remedies to collect the receivables. We recommended further that for fair presentation
of receivables, Management assess the actual status of dormant receivables; confirm
with existing debtors and request for write-off in accordance with Commission on
Audit Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if applicable.

Management was also enjoined to adhere with the general and specific guidelines
cited in COA Circular 2016-005 in order to minimize the incidence of receivables
falling past due.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to ensure that data on
discounts granted to taxpayers be clearly indicated during the preparation of collection
reports. We recommended further that the ICO - Municipal Accountant likewise
strictly comply with the accounting procedures prescribed in Section 22, Volume 1 of the
NGAS and COA Circular 2015-009 on the proper accounting treatment of discounts in
the collection of taxes.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
 Submit written justification for the incomplete supporting documents;
 Immediately submit the lacking documents as identified in Appendix A to avoid
suspension/ disallowance in audit or questions on the management of LGU
resources; and
 Henceforth, ensure the completeness of required supporting documents not only
in compliance with Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012 – 001
but most significantly provide backing that essential procedures were actually
undertaken prior to processing.

Considering the significance of timely submission of contracts and Purchase Orders,
we reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed to forward to the
Audit Team all perfected contracts and Purchase Orders along with all the required
attachments within five (5) days from perfection as provided under COA Circular No.
2009-001. This will enable the Audit Team to conduct timely evaluation of contracts and
review of items purchased and thereafter immediately communicate to management any
defects/deficiencies noted thereon.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct
the Accountable Officers concerned to immediately submit the financial reports along
with the transaction documents pertaining to disbursement such as but not limited to,
paid vouchers, official receipts, and corresponding supporting documents, else the Team
may be constrained to suspend/disallow the claims.

We further recommended that the Accountable Officers and Finance personnel
thereafter ensure utmost compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 39 of
P.D. 1445 to enable evaluation of the transactions’ propriety, completeness and accuracy
within a reasonable time from their occurrence.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
 Always require posting of warranty security in any acceptable form for
completed infrastructure projects in accordance with Section 62 of the 2016
IRR of RA 9184;
 Henceforth establish a system and maintain records on specific warranty
periods and security for each procurement of civil works.

We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inspectorate
Team to conduct inspection of completed projects after the one (1) year defects liability
period and issue the corresponding Certificate of Final Acceptance to the contractors if
no defect /deficiency was detected.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Municipal Engineer agreed to enforce
the Building Code and its IRR by requiring proof of conformity with the standards from
owners of existing structures and requisite applications from owners of buildings that
are yet to be constructed.

We, further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer support the information
dissemination of the regulatory fee and thereafter impose the same as authorized under
the Municipality’s 2011 Revenue Code.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to select from qualified
senior citizens and thereafter appoint an OSCA Head not only to fully comply with the
IRR of the Expanded Senior Citizens Act but most importantly, to fully support the
improvement of the total well-being of the elderly and motivate them to contribute to
nation building.

While we recognize the on-going efforts of the municipality for the operation and
maintenance of the SALINTUBIG project, we recommended and Management agreed
to:
 Require the WATSAN council to prepare a reasonable work plan for the
consideration of the Sangguniang Bayan in the provision of annual funding;
 Either assist the community to establish the WSPs through capacity building
activities for eventual turn-over or undertake ring-fencing; and
 Monitor and Evaluate the physical and financial aspects of operations including its
functionality, effectiveness and efficiency for the benefit of the intended end-users.

We recommended that in order to prevent further deterioration and/or eventual
wastage of resources, Management:
 Submit subsequent plan on the delivered materials which remained unutilized
from the date of delivery in 2013; and
 Henceforth enhance monitoring of projects undertaken with implementing
partners as well as strengthen coordination with barangay officials and
identified beneficiaries as deemed appropriate to ensure that these are
operationally useful as intended.

While we recognize the ongoing efforts of the municipality, we recommended that in
order to enjoy the benefits of effective Ecological Solid Waste Management and to avoid
possible sanctions, the members of the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board:
 Facilitate preliminary activities to ensure that the type of disposal should be
designed as sanitary landfill;
 Enforce waste segregation at source; and
 Monitor and encourage utmost compliance by its component barangays.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure availability of resources and
inclusion of the lacking items in the formulation of the LDRRMFIP in compliance with
the minimum preparedness standards set forth under the NDPP not only to support the
initiatives of the DILG but most significantly to avert the loss of lives during disasters.

 The members of the Inventory Committee conduct annual physical inventory and thereafter prepare and submit the RPCPPE for adjustment in the agency books/records, if necessary; or otherwise undertake a reconstitution of the committee;

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accounting Division personnel:

•	Facilitate the immediate submission of transaction documents and preparation of  financial reports that are already long overdue; 

•	Maintain and update the related journals and ledgers to facilitate the generation of timely financial reports/statements; and 

•	 Henceforth prepare and submit financial transaction documents and reports pertaining to subsequent periods within the deadlines specified in COA Circular 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Submitted those already overdue. Exerted efforts to submit on time
observations.reason: Delays in the submission were still observed, hence the issuance of another AOM

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that  the Municipal Accountant:

•	Immediately submit the lacking documents as identified in Appendix A to avoid suspension in audit; and 

•	Henceforth, ensure the completeness of required supporting documents in compliance with Section 4 (6) of P.D. 1445 and  Sections 9.1 and 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012 – 001 prior to  the processing of  claims.
observations.managementAction: Submitted all the lacking documents; Exerted efforts in the completion of supporting documents
observations.reason: Submission of lacking documents contained in the Appendix but observed incomplete supporting documents in subsequent transactions

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer:

•	Always require posting of warranty security in any acceptable form for goods delivered and infrastructures implemented; and

•	Henceforth establish a system and maintain records on specific warranty periods and security for each procurement of goods and civil works.
observations.managementAction: Required posting of warranties; started to establish a system to monitor
observations.reason: Observed imposition of warranties; Initial system for monitoring not  yet presented for validation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management formulate and adopt a CPES-based system of monitoring and contractors evaluation to ensure that infrastructure projects conform with the specified requirements of projects.
observations.managementAction: Started
observations.reason: Initial information presented

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management ensure the proper and uniform withholding of taxes not just to implement the revenue regulations but most importantly to avoid any sanction and/or penalty.  The practice of consistent compliance with tax laws and rules will   help the national government in the collection of much needed revenues.
observations.managementAction: Imposed strict withholding of taxes
observations.reason: No similar reportable condition noted as of validation date

observations.managementAction: Submitted to DENR on May 12, 2016
observations.reason: Copy of 10-year plan on file

observations.recommendation:
While we took cognizance of the relentless efforts of the LGU to support gender equality and women empowerment, we recommended that the Executive Committee and the Technical Working Group:

•	endeavor to  capacitate themselves  through attendance to trainings under GFPS  for a more responsive planning;
•	facilitate the updating of the 2010 GAD Code to make it more responsive; and
•	assess the extent of compliance through a compliance checklist of the elements of the institutional mechanisms in place and intensify efforts on those areas that should be in place.
observations.managementAction: Implemented the recommendation except for the completion of the database
observations.reason: Trainings for the members; Initial database only
